mission.title: Install and Configure Cdk For Kubernetes Cdk8s- on Kubernetes
mission.description: cdk8s is an open-source software development framework that allows you to define Kubernetes applications and abstractions using familiar programming languages and rich object-oriented APIs. Installing cdk8s enables you to synthesize Kubernetes manifests that can be applied to any Kubernetes cluster.

steps.title: Install Node.js and npm
steps.description:
cdk8s requires Node.js and npm. Install them using the following commands:

```bash
# For Ubuntu/Debian
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y nodejs npm

# For macOS using Homebrew
brew install node
```

steps.title: Install cdk8s CLI globally
steps.description:
Use npm to install the cdk8s command-line interface globally:

```bash
npm install -g cdk8s-cli@0.0.0
```

steps.title: Create a new cdk8s project
steps.description:
Create a new directory for your cdk8s project and initialize it:

```bash
mkdir my-cdk8s-app
cd my-cdk8s-app
cdk8s init --language=typescript
```

steps.title: Synthesize Kubernetes manifests
steps.description:
After defining your Kubernetes resources in the `main.ts` file, synthesize the Kubernetes manifests:

```bash
cdk8s synth
```

steps.title: Apply the synthesized manifests to your Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
Use kubectl to apply the synthesized manifests to your Kubernetes cluster:

```bash
kubectl apply -f dist/my-cdk8s-app.k8s.yaml --namespace my-namespace --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the resources are running in your Kubernetes cluster:

```bash
kubectl get all -n my-namespace
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of cdk8s will allow you to synthesize Kubernetes manifests and apply them to your cluster. You can verify the installation by checking the resources created in your specified namespace.
codeSnippets:
# For Ubuntu/Debian
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y nodejs npm

# For macOS using Homebrew
brew install node

mkdir my-cdk8s-app
cd my-cdk8s-app
cdk8s init --language=typescript

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl configured to interact with it.
